experiment: F-EVAL4 Event-Frequency Asymmetry in PHIL-14 Goal Metrics

pre_registration.hypothesis: PHIL-14 goals use data sources with vastly different event frequencies, creating asymmetric detection latency for behavioral regressions
pre_registration.expected: Protect/Truthful detection latency >100x Increase due to event-frequency gap
pre_registration.falsified_if: All four goals have comparable detection latency (<5x ratio)

method.approach: Measure events-per-session for each PHIL-14 goal's primary data source, compute detection latency (sessions needed to detect 10pp behavioral regression with p<0.05)
method.sample: N=421 sessions, N=19 challenges, N=36 active-file lanes

temporal_windowing_effect.note: Windowing changes rates but not verdicts in this snapshot. However, marginal sensitivity triples (0.26pp to 0.76pp), making future regressions detectable sooner.

findings: 40x event-frequency gap: Increase generates 1.84 events/session vs Protect/Truthful at 0.045/session
28x detection-latency asymmetry: a 10pp behavioral regression in Increase is detectable in 16 sessions; in Protect/Truthful, 444 sessions
Protect and Truthful share the same data source (challenges), creating correlated blind spots
Temporal windowing improves marginal sensitivity 3x (0.26pp to 0.76pp per event) but cannot overcome the fundamental event-frequency gap
The eval_sufficiency system is responsive to production regressions but nearly blind to ethical/epistemic regressions

structural_fix_proposal.principle: Every session must generate at least one observation for every PHIL-14 goal
structural_fix_proposal.mechanism: Add per-session protect/truthful observations to SESSION-LOG.md or eval_sufficiency.py: (1) was any belief corrected? (2) did any evidence contradict existing claims? (3) was validate_beliefs.py run and PASS?
structural_fix_proposal.expected_effect: Normalize event frequency across goals to >0.5/session, reducing max detection latency from 444 to <50 sessions
structural_fix_proposal.implementation_cost: Low — add 3 boolean fields to session-end logging

fourth_metric_design_property.description: All metrics in a composite scorer must have comparable event frequencies (within 5x) or the scorer has asymmetric sensitivity to different failure modes

verdict: CONFIRMED — 40x event-frequency gap creates 28x detection-latency asymmetry. Fourth metric-design property identified.
